title: Malaxis brevifolia
taxonomy: Malaxis brevifolia is a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Malaxis and the family Orchidaceae. The genus Malaxis is often included in the larger genus Platanthera. However, some sources still recognize Malaxis as a distinct genus. Malaxis brevifolia is native to North America.

intro: Malaxis brevifolia, also known as the short-leaved Malaxis or short-leaved orchid, is a species of terrestrial orchid native to the southeastern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Malaxis brevifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Malaxis brevifolia is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
